theme: delivery route driven blind through road closures
title: Blind Delivery Through Road Closures
seed_problem: robust vehicle routing under scenario-based edge closures with a fixed, order-blind replanning rule
mutation: composite: a fixed visiting-order (permutation) is scored not by its nominal length but by replaying it under K weighted road-closure scenarios via a FIXED deterministic detour rule (drive the shortest currently-open path to the next stop); the objective becomes a scenario-weighted sum of realized travel, coupling permutation design to a structural avoidance problem over the closure ensemble rather than a shortest-tour problem.
strategies: trivial: identity order 2,3,...,N (reproduces the checker's own baseline construction)

naive greedy: plain nearest-neighbour TSP construction on the raw, undisturbed road network, ignoring which roads any scenario would block -- repeatedly depends on scenario-fragile shortcuts because they look cheap in raw terms

insight (strong): price every road at raw length + LAMBDA * (sum of the weights of scenarios that block it), turning scenario risk into an edge cost; nearest-neighbour construction on this risk-adjusted graph naturally avoids repeatedly depending on heavily-threatened roads, then an exact-delta 2-opt local search refines the tour against the REAL scenario-weighted objective via precomputed per-scenario all-pairs distances

a general solver could go further: jointly optimise the crossing POSITION between structural regions against the full scenario-weight distribution (a weighted min-cut / exposure-minimisation view), or run simulated annealing directly on the real objective
